name: Agüeira River Trail
name_original: Ruta del río Agüeira — circular desde Pesoz
description: The Route of the Agüeira river circular from Pesoz is a moderate 4.7-mile hike through forests and traditional villages.
region_intro: Hiking around Pesoz offers access to a green and less-visited part of Asturias, characterized by river valleys and dense forests. The municipality is shaped by the confluence of the Agüeira and Navia rivers, contributing to a lush environment. Situated within the Oscos-Eo Biosphere Reserve, the area features gentle mountains and a network of trails through oak and chestnut woodlands. Best hiking trails around Pesoz The most popular hiking route is Agüeira River Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the course of the Agüeira River through forested terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the environments of Grandas - circular from Grandas de Salime, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This circular route explores the varied landscapes surrounding Grandas de Salime. Local hikers also love the Berducedo to Buspol, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Asturian countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Pesoz is defined by river valleys, extensive forests of oak and chestnut, and gentle mountain terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Pesoz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Pesoz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Pesoz?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Pesoz, offering a variety of experiences. The region is known for its lush river valleys and dense forests, providing a tranquil escape for nature lovers.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Pesoz?
A: The majority of hikes in Pesoz are considered &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt;, with 8 routes falling into this category. There are also 2 &lt;em&gt;easy&lt;/em&gt; trails available, making the area accessible for various fitness levels. No difficult routes are listed in the immediate vicinity.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Pesoz?
A: The trails in Pesoz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the area's quiet woodlands, scenic river valleys, and the blend of natural beauty with historical insights.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints to discover on hikes near Pesoz?
A: Yes, Pesoz is rich in natural beauty. You can experience the confluence of the Agüeira and Navia rivers, and explore the lush environment of the Oscos-Eo Biosphere Reserve, known for its gentle mountains and forests. A prominent viewpoint is the &lt;strong&gt;Mirador de A Paicega&lt;/strong&gt;, offering spectacular views of the Navia River canyon and surrounding sierras, accessible via the Ruta a A Paicega.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I find along the hiking routes?
A: Many routes in Pesoz offer a glimpse into the region's history. The Ruta a A Paicega, for instance, leads to &lt;strong&gt;A Paicega&lt;/strong&gt;, an abandoned worker village with remnants of old barracks and a church. Along this trail, you can also observe historical 'cuerrias' (circular stone structures for storing chestnuts). Additionally, you might encounter historical buildings like the Palacio de los Ron in Pesoz itself.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Pesoz?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047545115/route-of-the-environments-of-grandas-circular-from-grandas-de-salime" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the environments of Grandas - circular from Grandas de Salime&lt;/a&gt;, which explores varied landscapes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047547680/route-of-mon-and-green-path-of-san-martin-de-oscos-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of Mon and Green Path of San Martín de Oscos — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Pesoz suitable for families?
A: Yes, some trails are suitable for families. The Ruta a A Paicega, for example, is considered easy and suitable for families, offering a blend of nature and history. The area's generally moderate difficulty and lush, shaded paths make it pleasant for walks with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Pesoz?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, the natural and less-visited character of Pesoz, with its extensive forests and river paths, generally makes it a welcoming area for dog walking. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and respect local wildlife and other hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pesoz?
A: Pesoz, being part of Asturias, offers a lush green environment. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the shaded forest paths, though it can be warmer. Winter hikes are possible, but some paths might be wet or muddy.

Q: Are there any attractions nearby that I can visit after a hike?
A: Absolutely! After your hike, you can explore several nearby attractions. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Marina de Buspol Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and its associated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7034965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salime Dam&lt;/a&gt;. There are also charming religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María Magdalena de La Mesa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Mary of Berducedo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Pesoz?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, Pesoz is a small municipality. Many trails, like the Ruta a A Paicega, start directly in the village, suggesting that parking would be available within or on the outskirts of Pesoz. For routes further afield, local parking areas are typically found near villages or popular starting points.
tips: Pesoz offers elements of historical and architectural interest, as well as a museum facility worth visiting, the Ethnological Museum of Pesoz. You can visit the church of Santiago, whose original walls are still standing, although the rest of the temple dates from later centuries. Another very evocative medieval element is the nearby tower, inside the Ron palace complex, a lineage of great power several centuries ago. The palace is an example of Baroque palace architecture during the 17th and 18th centuries and features a central courtyard and two towers on its main façade.

The Ron Palace was built in the 18th century by the Ron family, one of the most important in the area at that time. It is in the Baroque style and has several bodies, highlighting the tower that crowns the building. On its main façade, the doorway with a semicircular arch and a balcony with a wrought iron railing stands out. Inside, the palace has a chapel, several noble rooms and a carved stone staircase. Currently, the Palacio de Ron is used as a cultural center and hosts temporary exhibitions and other cultural activities.

faqs.question: Where does the Route of the Agüeira river trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts and ends in the village of Pesoz, Asturias. You'll find parking options within Pesoz itself, making it convenient to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: While komoot estimates the route at around 2 hours and 29 minutes, most hikers find it takes between 3 to 5 hours to complete, allowing for stops to enjoy the scenery and historical sites. This accounts for its moderate difficulty and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Route of the Agüeira river?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, characteristic of a moderate hike. You'll encounter descents and ascents through lush forests, particularly chestnuts and pines, follow riverbanks, and traverse meadows and cultivated land. Expect natural paths, some potentially uneven, and a wooden walkway near the Ahío River.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Agüeira river trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through natural areas and potentially near livestock or other wildlife, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect the local environment.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally accessible year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal. Summer can be warm but the forest sections provide good shade. Winter hiking is also possible, but be prepared for potentially cooler and wetter conditions.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural beauty and cultural heritage. You'll pass through the singular village of Argul, a declared Site of Cultural Interest, and the caserío of Sequeiros with its traditional structures. In Pesoz, you can see the Romanesque Church of Santiago de Pesoz and the historic Palacio de Ron. There are also remnants of an old mill near the Ahío River.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits, nor are there any entrance fees to hike the Route of the Agüeira river. While the trail is located within the ZEC Cuenca del Agüeira, it is freely accessible to the public. Always remember to respect the natural environment and any local regulations.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with or intersect other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Route of the Agüeira river (PR-AS 117) largely follows the official "Ruta del Augüeira" for about 99% of its course. It also briefly intersects with sections of the "Road Oscos, Cuenca del Navia y Sierra de Penouta" trail for about 18% of its length.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Agüeira river suitable for beginner hikers or families with children?
faqs.answer: Classified as moderate, the trail involves descents and ascents, requiring good physical fitness. While not overly strenuous, it might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. Families with some hiking experience should be fine, but be prepared for a few hours of walking with elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Pesoz, where you may find some local establishments. As you hike through the more rural sections and hamlets like Sequeiros and Argul, facilities will be limited, so it's best to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Route of the Agüeira river?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device (like komoot's app) is also advisable, especially for navigating through the forested sections.
